title: Glyceria phryganodes
taxonomy: Glyceria phryganodes is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Meliceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as spikerushes or wood reeds. Within the genus Glyceria, Glyceria phryganodes is further classified as a type of marsh grass. The exact classification of this species may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a member of the plant kingdom. More research is needed to fully understand the classification of Glyceria phryganodes.
